title: Imperial Hyperspace Security Net
content:
{{Top|ffa|canon=Hyperspace Security Net}}
{{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}}

{{Quote|We're entering the Deep Core Security Zone, planet Byss has acknowledged our approach…|[[Shug Ninx]]|audio=ShugNinx-DE.flac|<ref name="Dark Empire (audio)">[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
The '''Imperial Hyperspace Security Net''' was an overlapping network of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] disruption technologies used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to control hyperspace traffic through the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. As a response to [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hit-and-run attacks on Imperial convoys and [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], the Security Net was installed across common [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace routes]] throughout the Deep Core to give Imperial strongholds advanced warning of approaching enemy [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]]. The Security Net utilized non-mass [[Hyperwave transceiver/Legends|hyperwave transceivers]] emitting cross-channeled [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] to detect incoming [[Starship/Legends|starships]] traveling through hyperspace. Once a starship was detected, [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] were brought online to drop the ship out of hyperspace and prevent it from escaping. The ship then had to present the proper registration code to the local planetary security forces or else be fired upon by surrounding Imperial ships. [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] was one of the many Imperial planets that used the Security Net during the Empire's resurgence in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. After the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s usurpation of the Imperial throne in [[137 ABY]], the Security Net was expanded to include the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and utilized [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] strewn across common hyperspace routes to monitor hyperspace traffic.

==Description==
{{Quote|Rumor has it that the Security Net has interdictor ships, minefields, and entire starfleets patrolling the few routes in. If you ask me, anything the Emperor wants to guard that badly? He can have it.|Grinner|<ref name="EotE">[[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Core Rulebook]]</ref>}}
[[File:ByssSecurity.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Hyperwave transceivers allowed planetary security forces to detect ships before they dropped out of hyperspace.]]
The Imperial Hyperspace Security Net was a system of [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] and non-mass [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] [[Hyperwave transceiver/Legends|transceivers]] utilized as an early warning system by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to detect [[Starship/Legends|starships]] entering the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. The Security Net was able to give Imperial [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]—and with some refinements, starships—in the Deep Core advanced warning of ships approaching through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. It was able to do this by using a system of non-mass [[Hyperwave/Legends|hyperwave]] transceivers, originally developed for the HoloNet to pass communications in specific frequencies, linked by a network of coordinated [[Simu-tunnel/Legends|S-threads]]. The transceivers projected cross-channeled [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]], which registered a hyperspace disturbance whenever a ship passed through it. The transceivers were able to detect these disturbances at cosmically short distances and signal the [[Gravity well/Legends|gravity well]] projectors to drop incoming ships out of hyperspace.<ref name="DESB" />

[[Gravity/Legends|Gravity]] well projectors served as the backbone of the Security Net, creating artificial [[Mass shadow/Legends|mass shadows]] that pulled ships into [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] and prevented them from escaping.<ref name="DESB" /> Sometimes [[Gravity mine/Legends|gravity mines]] or [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruisers]] were used in place of, or in addition to, gravity well projectors. Once a ship was pulled out of hyperspace, it had to present proper authorization to [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] officials or else be fired upon by any surrounding [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and support craft. All ships wishing to pass through the Security Net had be registered with the [[Bureau of Ships and Services/Legends|Bureau of Ships and Services]] and have [[Imperial Security Authorization]] and [[Imperial Intelligence Clearance]].<ref name="BDC">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050414planetintro|text=Byss and the Deep Core|int=Byss and the Deep Core}}</ref>

Security Net technology was incredibly bulky and expensive. Sections of the Security Net could have been built around systems, but practically they only covered the most common hyperspace entry [[Hyperspace route/Legends|routes]]. Even then, the Security Net had the potential to have many holes in it and thereby be subverted through the use of experimental routes. Some transceivers were outfitted with [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] and [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computers]], allowing them to randomly jump from point to point and cover more space. Ships unaware of the jump patterns of these transceivers often found it difficult to penetrate the [[Deep Core Security Zone/Legends|Deep Core Security Zone]] undetected. Any system using the Security Net often rounded out its security with patrol vessels, [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Mine/Legends|mines]], [[antimatter]] particles, and [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]].<ref name="DESB" /> By [[137 ABY]], use of the Security Net had branched out to include the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. In addition to gravity mines and [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Flotilla/Legends|flotillas]], the updated Security Net consisted of [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] placed along common hyperspace routes where random inspections were performed on ships entering and leaving the Deep Core and the Core Worlds.<ref name="LECG" />

==History==
{{Quote|These days, nobody is allowed to go there. The Empire has set up this thing called the Imperial Hyperspace Security Net to make sure no one gets into the Deep Core Security Zone without his mighty majesty's permission.|Grinner|<ref name="EotE" />}}
The Imperial Hyperspace Security Net was constructed in response to surprise ambushes on Imperial [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]] and [[Planet/Legends|planets]] by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] ships. Alliance tacticians such as [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] perfected the use of hit-and-run skirmishes on Imperial ships through careful use of the hyperdrive, forcing the Empire to stop sending out unarmed [[Transport/Legends|transports]]. Full-scale Alliance [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] dropping out of hyperspace also had the potential to devastate key locations on an Imperial world before planetary defenses were able to be brought online. The Security Net was installed throughout the Deep Core to give Imperial worlds advanced warning of approaching enemy fleets.<ref name="DESB" />

The technology used to create artificial gravity wells was developed after an accidental discovery by Imperial [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] in the [[Sedri system/Legends|Sedri system]]. The scientists discovered that it was possible to create an artificial gravity well, and though their mission in the system was foiled by agents of the Rebellion, the scientists learned much. After several months of research, they were able to learn how to create an artificial gravity well on their own. Gravity well generators were soon installed on the Empire's newly constructed Interdictor [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] and, eventually, were used in the Security Net. The introduction of this technology caused the Rebels to become more cautious about attacking Imperial convoys and worlds.<ref name="DESB" /> The Security Net was soon installed around Imperial planets in the Deep Core such as the [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]].<ref name="Endnotes" />

[[File:SlaveIIFail.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Quick response by Byss Security prevented ''Slave II'' from penetrating Byss's planetary shield.]]
The Security Net was not without its weaknesses, however. When the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] lost control of the Deep Core world [[Vulpter/Legends|Vulpter]] after the formation of the Empire, the shipping of foodstuffs to the planet was halted due to the Empire's anti-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Speciesism|bias]]. Vulpter, cut-off from the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] after the establishment of the Deep Core Security Zone, became a safe haven for Rebels and criminals who were able to bypass the Security Net.<ref name="BDC4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050428planet4|series=Byss and the Deep Core|text=Part 4: Vulpter}}</ref> Although [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] wished to conceal the Deep Core from the rest of the galaxy behind a veil of secrecy through its inaccessibility, he still needed supplies and [[Troop/Legends|troops]] brought into the region. [[Deep Core hauler]]s, [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captains]] hired by the Empire, carried out this task and were some of the few individuals outside of the Empire given clearance to pass through the Security Net.<ref name="DESB" />

One of these Deep Core haulers was [[Salla Zend]], a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] whose freighter, the ''[[Starlight Intruder]]'', was licensed to transport goods beyond the Security Net to Byss.<ref name="DESB" /> After Palpatine's [[Cloning/Legends|resurrection]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Zend attempted to smuggle her old flame, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], along with his wife, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], into Byss to rescue Organa Solo's [[Twin/Legends|twin]] brother, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. ''En route'' to Byss, the ''Intruder'' was followed by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who intended to collect a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Solo. Thanks to the Security Net, [[Byss Security]] was able to track the ''Intruder'' as it exited hyperspace and allowed it safe passage through Byss's [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]]. Byss Security also noticed Fett's approach right behind the ''Intruder'' and, after receiving no authorization from him, was able to close the planetary [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] quickly, causing Fett's ship, ''[[Slave II]]'', to crash into it with devastating effects.<ref name="DE4">[[Dark Empire 4|''Dark Empire'' 4]]</ref>

At some point, the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Grinner]] wrote a memo about [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] opportunities within the Deep Core, claiming that the Security Net made it incredibly difficult to make runs through the area.<ref name="EotE" /> By 137 ABY, the Security Net had expanded to include the Core Worlds. With the addition of space stations along hyperspace routes, the Empire, now controlled by the [[One Sith]] under the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]], was able to perform random inspections on starships and stop attacks against protected planets.<ref name="LECG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Imperial Hyperspace Security Net was first mentioned in [[Tom Veitch]]'s [[Dark Empire endnotes|endnotes]] from the [[Dark Empire 2|second issue]] of the [[1991]]–[[1992]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]''. It first appeared in the [[Dark Empire 4|fourth issue]] of ''Dark Empire'', as well as the comic's [[Dark Empire (audio drama)|audio adaptation]]. The Security Net was first detailed in [[West End Games]]' ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' and later in several [[Wizards of the Coast]] sources such as ''[[Byss and the Deep Core]]'' and the ''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''.
